paper.title: Particle acceleration at the bow shock of runaway star LS 2355: non-thermal radio emission but no $\gamma$-ray counterpart

extraction.summary: The paper studies the bow shock of LS 2355, a runaway massive star, but does not present it as a Galactic-unbound or HVS candidate. The paper derives a stellar velocity v* = 7.0 +/- 2.5 km/s relative to the local ISM and focuses on the non-thermal radio emission from the bow shock. No bound/unbound assessment or escape-velocity comparison is performed, and no objects are discussed as possibly escaping the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The runaway massive star LS 2355 (HD 99897 / HIP 56021 / Gaia DR3 5333860240705973888) and its bow shock, the sole stellar object studied in the paper.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper consistently describes LS 2355 as a runaway massive star ejected from its birth location, but it never assesses whether the star could escape the Milky Way or presents it as a hyper-velocity-star / Galactic-escape candidate. The derived velocity (v* = 7.0 +/- 2.5 km/s relative to the local ISM) is only marginally supersonic and is discussed in the context of bow-shock modelling, not escape from the Galactic potential.

source_refs.context: Paper title identifies LS 2355 as a runaway star, not an HVS or unbound candidate
Abstract discusses the bow shock driven by LS 2355 and its updated proper motion and velocity; no mention of Galactic escape or unbound status
Introduction describes runaway massive stars as ejected from birth locations via dynamical interactions or supernova of a binary companion; no claim that LS 2355 is unbound from the Galaxy
Gaia-derived stellar velocity v* = 7.0 +/- 2.5 km/s is presented as an updated proper-motion measurement for bow-shock modelling; no bound/unbound assessment is made
